authors: Boshi Wang, Weijian Xu, Yunsheng Li, Mei Gao, Yujia Xie, Huan Sun, Dongdong Chen
title: Improving Code Localization with Repository Memory
storage_format: Corpus of recent commits (episodic: messages + patches + linked issues, searchable via BM25 with an LLM tokenizer) + functionality summaries of active files (semantic: NL text generated by LLM for the top-200 most-edited files)
agent_framework: LocAgent (localization framework based on a heterogeneous graph)
benchmarks_used: SWE-bench-verified (500 examples, 12 repositories), SWE-bench-live (130 examples, 62 repositories)
memory_size_over_time: 7000 commits + 200 most-edited files per repository; size fixed by design (fixed window of recent commits)
cost_per_task: $0.58-$0.89/example (RepoMem) vs $0.54-$0.59/example (LocAgent); higher cost concentrated on hard problems where LocAgent fails
main_result: Acc@5 76.5% on SWE-bench-verified (+4.9% vs LocAgent); episodic and semantic memory contribute in a complementary manner; the agent shifts from brute-force exploration to hypothesis-driven investigation
performance_vs_baseline: +4.9% absolute Acc@5 vs LocAgent on SWE-bench-verified; +3.1% on SWE-bench-live; resolve rate +3.4% end-to-end (40.4% vs 37.0%)
cost_vs_baseline: Higher cost on hard problems ($0.54→$0.89 when LocAgent fails); high per-example variance; memory is a strategic investment on hard problems
limitations: Performance degrades on repositories with limited history (<10K commits); variable, problem-dependent cost; retrieval method not optimized for code vocabulary; explicit links between commits and issues may be noisy or absent
validity_threats: Evaluation only on bug fixing/localization; GPT-4o as the sole model; pre-issue commits may have variable quality; the fixed 7K-commit window may not be ideal for every repository
